retry_reason: Allocation failure is transient, but the function does not expose a return value to the caller and does not retry internally. The typical kernel pattern is to bail out; retry is not part of the semantic contract.

judge.reason: The function's primary purpose is to allocate and submit a firmware event to turn on a PFA LED. The refcount operations (set via alloc_fw_event_work, put via fw_event_work_put) are internal lifecycle management for the event object, not the main goal. The critical variable (fw_event) is not passed to the caller — it is consumed internally. This is an event-submission helper, not a reference-count wrapper.
